questions.question: Who were the main European trading companies that came to India?
questions.correct_answer: The Portuguese, Dutch, French and British trading companies.
questions.explanation: These European powers came to India mainly for trade and later competed for political control.

questions.question: What was the East India Company?
questions.correct_answer: A British trading company that came to India for trade.
questions.explanation: Over time it expanded its power and began ruling large parts of India.

questions.question: When was the East India Company established?
questions.explanation: The British government granted it the right to trade with the East.

questions.question: What was the main aim of the East India Company in India?
questions.correct_answer: To trade in valuable goods like silk, cotton, spices and indigo.
questions.explanation: These goods were highly demanded in Europe.

questions.question: What was a 'factory' in the context of the East India Company?
questions.correct_answer: A place where the Company stored goods and carried out trade.
questions.explanation: Factories were trading posts established by European companies.

questions.question: Who was Siraj-ud-Daulah?
questions.correct_answer: The Nawab of Bengal who opposed the East India Company.
questions.explanation: He fought against the British during the Battle of Plassey.

questions.question: When did the Battle of Plassey take place?
questions.explanation: This battle marked the beginning of British political control in India.

questions.question: Who led the British forces in the Battle of Plassey?
questions.explanation: His victory helped the East India Company gain control over Bengal.

questions.question: What was the Battle of Buxar?
questions.correct_answer: A battle fought in 1764 between the East India Company and Indian rulers.
questions.explanation: The Company defeated the combined forces of the Nawab of Bengal, Nawab of Awadh and the Mughal Emperor.

questions.question: What were Diwani rights?
questions.correct_answer: The right to collect revenue from Bengal, Bihar and Orissa.
questions.explanation: The East India Company received these rights from the Mughal Emperor in 1765.

notes.title: East India Company
notes.content: The British East India Company started as a trading company but gradually became a political power controlling large territories in India.

notes.title: Battle of Plassey
notes.content: The Battle of Plassey in 1757 allowed the British to gain control over Bengal and marked the beginning of British rule in India.

notes.title: Battle of Buxar
notes.content: The victory at Buxar in 1764 strengthened the political power of the East India Company in India.

notes.title: Diwani Rights
notes.content: Diwani rights allowed the East India Company to collect revenue, giving it strong economic and administrative control.

notes.title: Expansion of British Power
notes.content: Through wars, alliances and administrative control, the East India Company gradually expanded its rule across India.

facts.fact: The East India Company was established in 1600.
The Battle of Plassey in 1757 marked the beginning of British rule in India.
The Battle of Buxar took place in 1764.
The Company received Diwani rights in 1765.
